perturbation.original_text: (b) Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information of Disclosing Party in accordance with a judicial or other governmental order, provided that Receiving Party either (i) gives the undersigned Disclosing Party reasonable notice prior to such disclosure to allow Disclosing Party a reasonable opportunity to seek a protective order or equivalent, or (ii) obtains written assurance from the applicable judicial or governmental entity that it will afford the Confidential Information the highest level of protection afforded under applicable law or regulation. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party shall not disclose any computer source code that contains Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party in accordance with a judicial or other governmental order unless it complies with the requirement set forth in sub-section (i) of this Section 2(b).
perturbation.changed_text: (b) Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information of Disclosing Party in accordance with a judicial or other governmental order, provided that Receiving Party either (i) gives the undersigned Disclosing Party reasonable notice prior to such disclosure, or (ii) obtains written assurance from the applicable judicial or governmental entity. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party shall not disclose any computer source code that contains Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party unless it complies with the requirement set forth in sub-section (i) of this Section 2(b).
perturbation.explanation: The omission of 'to allow Disclosing Party a reasonable opportunity to seek a protective order or equivalent' from (i) significantly weakens the protection afforded to the Disclosing Party. The omission of 'that it will afford the Confidential Information the highest level of protection afforded under applicable law or regulation' from (ii) further erodes this protection, as it removes the requirement for the judicial or governmental entity to provide a high level of protection. The omission of 'in accordance with a judicial or other governmental order' from the last sentence, broadens the restriction on disclosing computer source code even if the source code is under a judicial order.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties acknowledge that monetary damages may not be a sufficient remedy for unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information and that Disclosing Party shall be entitled, without waiving any other rights or remedies, to such injunctive or equitable relief as may be deemed proper by a court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The original text provided a mechanism for the disclosing party to seek a protective order or assurance of a high level of protection. The changed text removes these protections, weakening the disclosing party's ability to prevent unauthorized disclosure. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that monetary damages may not be sufficient and the disclosing party is entitled to injunctive or equitable relief. By weakening the protection against unauthorized disclosure, the changed text undermines the basis for seeking injunctive relief and creates a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (d) Receiving Party shall notify the undersigned Disclosing Party immediately upon discovery of any unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information or any other breach of this Agreement by Receiving Party and its employees and consultants, and will cooperate with Disclosing Party in every reasonable way to help Disclosing Party regain possession of the Confidential Information and prevent its further unauthorized use or disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: (d) Receiving Party shall notify the undersigned Disclosing Party upon discovery of any unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information or any other breach of this Agreement by Receiving Party, and will cooperate with Disclosing Party to prevent its further unauthorized use or disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the words 'immediately' and 'and its employees and consultants', the sense of urgency is gone and no one else is being held accountable, which is contradictory with Section 2(c). Also the removal of 'in every reasonable way to help Disclosing Party regain possession of the Confidential Information and' reduces the scope of cooperation required, potentially making it less effective.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) The undersigned Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information only to Receiving Party's employees and consultants on a need-to-know basis. The undersigned Receiving Party will have executed or shall execute appropriate written agreements with its employees and consultants sufficient to enable Receiving Party to enforce all the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the word 'immediately' and 'and its employees and consultants' from the original text. Section 2(c) explicitly states that the Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information only to Receiving Party's employees and consultants. The omission of 'and its employees and consultants' in the changed text of section (d), directly contradicts with section 2(c) because it removes accountability for employees and consultants, creating a conflict within the document as explained in the perturbation explanation. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: (f) If either Disclosing Party or the Receiving Party employs attorneys to enforce any rights arising out of or relating to this Agreement, the prevailing party shall be entitled to recover reasonable attorneys' fees and costs. This Agreement shall be construed and controlled by the laws of the People's Republic of China, and the parties further consent to submit any disputation arising from and/or in connection with this Agreement to China International Economic & Trade Arbitration Commission (CIETAC) for arbitration in Beijing in accordance with the Commission's arbitration rules in effect at the time of applying for arbitration. The arbitral award is final and binding upon both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: (f) If either Disclosing Party or the Receiving Party employs attorneys to enforce any rights arising out of or relating to this Agreement, the prevailing party shall be entitled to recover attorneys' fees. This Agreement shall be construed and controlled by the laws of the People's Republic of China, and the parties further consent to submit any disputation arising from and/or in connection with this Agreement to arbitration. The arbitral award is final and binding upon both parties.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the word 'reasonable' before the attorneys' fees, it implies that there is no limitation to how much the attorneys can charge, which is contradictory to what the other party thinks is a good cost to pay. Also, the removal of 'China International Economic & Trade Arbitration Commission (CIETAC) for arbitration in Beijing in accordance with the Commission's arbitration rules in effect at the time of applying for arbitration' creates uncertainty about the arbitration process, potentially leading to disputes over the rules and location of arbitration. This conflicts with the intention of having a clear and binding arbitration agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (e) This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. It shall not be modified except by a written agreement dated subsequent to the date of this Agreement and signed by both parties. None of the provisions of this Agreement shall be deemed to have been waived by any act or acquiescence on the part of Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party, their agents, or employees, but only by an instrument in writing signed by an authorized employee of Disclosing Party and the Receiving Party. No waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall constitute a waiver of any other provision(s) or of the same provision on another occasion.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits 'reasonable' from 'attorneys' fees,' implying unlimited fees, contradicting the agreement's intention for controlled costs. Also, the removal of specific arbitration details (CIETAC, Beijing, rules) introduces uncertainty. The contradicted text states that the agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and can only be modified by a subsequent written agreement, signed by both parties. The unilateral change in the modified text regarding attorneys' fees and arbitration details conflicts with the requirement for mutual agreement and written modification, creating a direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Suggestions and Feedback The Receiving Party may from time to time provide suggestions, comments or other feedback ("Feedback") to the Disclosing Party with respect to Confidential Information provided originally by the Disclosing Party. Both parties agree that all Feedback is and shall be given entirely voluntarily. Feedback, even if designated as confidential by the party offering the Feedback, shall not, absent a separate written agreement, create any confidentiality obligation for the receiver of the Feedback. Receiving Party will not give Feedback that is subject to license terms that seek to require any Disclosing Party product, technology, service or documentation incorporating or derived from such Feedback, or any Disclosing Party intellectual property, to be licensed or otherwise shared with any third party. Furthermore, except as otherwise provided herein or in a separate subsequent written agreement between the parties, the receiver of the Feedback shall be free to use, disclose, reproduce, license or otherwise distribute, and exploit the Feedback provided to it as it sees fit, entirely without obligation or restriction of any kind on account of intellectual property rights or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Suggestions and Feedback The Receiving Party may provide suggestions or other feedback ("Feedback") to the Disclosing Party with respect to Confidential Information. Both parties agree that all Feedback shall be given voluntarily. Feedback shall not create any confidentiality obligation for the receiver of the Feedback. Furthermore, the receiver of the Feedback shall be free to use the Feedback provided to it as it sees fit, entirely without obligation or restriction of any kind on account of intellectual property rights or otherwise.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the words `from time to time`, `comments`, `provided originally by the Disclosing Party`, `even if designated as confidential by the party offering the Feedback, shall not, absent a separate written agreement,` and `Receiving Party will not give Feedback that is subject to license terms that seek to require any Disclosing Party product, technology, service or documentation incorporating or derived from such Feedback, or any Disclosing Party intellectual property, to be licensed or otherwise shared with any third party` creates a contradiction when reffering to 4(d).
perturbation.contradicted_text: The terms of confidentiality under this Agreement shall not be construed to limit either the Disclosing Party or the Receiving Party's right to independently develop or acquire products without use of the other party's <PAGE> Appendix J to Exhibit A Confidential Information. Further, the Receiving Party shall be free to use for any purpose the residuals resulting from access to or work with the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party, provided that the Receiving Party shall not disclose the Confidential Information except as expressly permitted pursuant to the terms of this Agreement. The term "residuals" means information in intangible form, which is retained in memory by persons who have had access to the Confidential Information, including ideas, concepts, know-how or techniques contained therein. The Receiving Party shall not have any obligation to limit or restrict the assignment of such persons or to pay royalties for any work resulting from the use of residuals. However, this sub-paragraph shall not be deemed to grant to the Receiving Party a license under the Disclosing Party's copyrights or patents.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that Feedback, even if designated as confidential, does not create a confidentiality obligation absent a separate written agreement. It also states that the receiving party will not provide feedback that requires Disclosing Party to license Intellectual Property to a third party. The changed text removes these clauses. Removing the exception regarding confidentiality and the clause on licensing requirements create a contradiction with the Contradicted Text (4(d)), which allows Receiving party to independently develop or acquire products using residuals and techniques contained in the Confidential Information, even if those techniques are contained in feedback. Since the Changed Text removes the Disclosing Party's guarantee that Feedback will not require licensing of its IP, it creates a contradiction regarding the Disclosing Party's ability to freely use residuals, as the removed clause might have protected the disclosing party's intellectual property rights from the receiving party.
